hero.subtitle: Compare anything. From gadgets to fruits, concepts to software. Discover the relationship, key differences, and which one is right for you.

methodology.label: Methodology
methodology.title: How Our Comparisons Are Generated
methodology.pipelineDesc: Every comparison goes through a rigorous 4-phase process designed to produce balanced, evidence-based analysis.
methodology.phase1Desc: We search the web across 5-8 different angles per entity, gathering information from official sources, reviews, benchmarks, and expert analysis.
methodology.phase2Desc: An AI architect analyzes the relationship between the two entities and generates 4-6 comparison dimensions specifically tailored to them. No generic templates.
methodology.phase3Desc: Each dimension is analyzed independently with scores on a 0-10 scale. Each analysis cites 1-2 web sources that directly support the findings.
methodology.phase4Desc: A final phase extracts pros and cons for each entity and produces an actionable recommendation with a clear verdict.
methodology.dataSource1: Web search via multiple query angles covering factual overviews, technical specifications, expert reviews, and recent news
methodology.dataSource2: Each claim in the analysis is linked to its original source URL for transparency
methodology.dataSource3: Scores are based on publicly available benchmarks, reviews, and specifications
methodology.scoring1: All scores use a 0-10 scale where 10 represents the most favorable outcome
methodology.scoring2: For negative dimensions (e.g., risk, cost), lower real-world values receive higher scores
methodology.scoring3: Scores are relative within the comparison, not absolute ratings
methodology.editorial1: Comparisons are AI-generated and reviewed by the CompareAI Editorial Team
methodology.editorial2: Featured comparisons undergo quality review before publication
methodology.editorial3: Our methodology is continuously updated as AI capabilities improve
methodology.limitation1: AI analysis may contain inaccuracies — always verify critical decisions with primary sources
methodology.limitation2: Scores are relative within each comparison and should not be compared across reports
methodology.limitation3: Data freshness depends on available web sources at the time of generation

about.label: About
about.title: About CompareAI
about.whatWeDoDesc: CompareAI is a free AI-powered comparison engine that analyzes any two entities — products, concepts, technologies, services, or ideas. Our multi-agent AI pipeline uses web research to produce factual, source-backed comparisons with dimension-by-dimension scoring, pros and cons, and actionable recommendations.
about.whyDesc: Comparison searches are among the most common decision-making queries on the web. Existing comparison tools often lack depth, structured analysis, and source transparency. We built CompareAI to provide AI-powered comparisons that are backed by real web sources — not just LLM opinions.
about.teamDesc: CompareAI is built and maintained by the CompareAI Editorial Team. We combine AI engineering expertise with editorial rigor to deliver reliable comparison reports.
about.editorial1: Every featured comparison is reviewed for accuracy and completeness
about.editorial2: Sources are automatically collected from web research and linked directly in reports
about.editorial3: We prioritize factual, verifiable claims over subjective opinions
about.editorial4: Reports are updated when significant new information becomes available
about.contactDesc: For questions, feedback, or partnership inquiries, please reach out via our website.

feedback.question: Was this comparison helpful?
